sorting-direction.label: Sorting Direction (ASC, DESC)
sorting-direction.summary: Control sort order with ASC and DESC keywords.

sketch-asc-desc-intro.title: Sorting with ASC and DESC
sketch-asc-desc-intro.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, you can control the order of your query results using the `ORDER BY` clause. By default, results are sorted in ascending order (from smallest to largest, or A to Z). To specify the direction, use `ASC` for ascending or `DESC` for descending.

For example, to list products from the `products` table by price from lowest to highest:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products
ORDER BY price ASC;
```

To list products by price from highest to lowest:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC;
```

You can also sort by text columns (alphabetically) or by dates.

quiz-single-choice-default-order.title: Default Sorting Direction
quiz-single-choice-default-order.prompt: If you write `ORDER BY price` in a SQL query, which direction will the results be sorted by default?
quiz-single-choice-default-order.hint: What happens if you don't specify ASC or DESC?

help.concept: In SQL, the default sorting direction for ORDER BY is ascending unless you specify DESC.
help.hint_1: Think about the natural order for numbers or text.
help.hint_2: If you want descending order, you must write DESC explicitly.

quiz-multi-choice-which-keywords.title: Recognizing Sorting Keywords
quiz-multi-choice-which-keywords.prompt: Which of the following keywords can you use to control the sorting direction in an ORDER BY clause?
quiz-multi-choice-which-keywords.hint: Two of these are used to set direction.

help.concept: ASC and DESC are the keywords that specify ascending and descending order in SQL.
help.hint_1: Look for keywords that relate to direction.
help.hint_2: ORDER and GROUP are not used for sorting direction.

quiz-drag-reorder-price.title: Arrange by Price (Descending)
quiz-drag-reorder-price.prompt: Drag these product prices into the order they would appear if you sorted the products by `price DESC`.

help.concept: DESC sorts from highest to lowest value.
help.hint_1: Look for the largest number first.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.title: Fill in the Sorting Keyword
quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.prompt:
Complete the SQL statement to sort products by `created_at` from newest to oldest:

```sql
SELECT name, created_at FROM products
ORDER BY created_at [___];
```
quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.hint: Which keyword sorts from highest (latest) to lowest (earliest)?

help.concept: the missing term is used to sort values in descending order, such as newest to oldest dates.
help.hint_1: Think about which keyword means 'reverse' order.
help.hint_2: ASC is for ascending; the missing term is for descending.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.template:
SELECT name, created_at FROM products
ORDER BY created_at [___];

quiz-code-input-sort-by-stock-asc.title: Sort Products by Stock (Ascending)
quiz-code-input-sort-by-stock-asc.prompt: Write a SQL query to list all product names and their stock, sorted by stock in ascending order.
quiz-code-input-sort-by-stock-asc.hint: Use ORDER BY with the stock column and specify the direction.

help.concept: ORDER BY lets you sort results by a column. ASC sorts from smallest to largest.
help.hint_1: Remember to select both name and stock columns.
help.hint_2: Add ORDER BY stock ASC at the end of your query.
